Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. These findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria and possible glycemic dysregulation. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
